name: Axios npm Supply Chain Compromise
description: Malicious axios versions published via hijacked maintainer account (jasonsaayman). Cross-platform RAT with credential-harvesting and self-destruct capability.

safe_versions.notes: axios@1.14.0 and earlier are safe; 1.14.1 is malicious; 0.30.3 and earlier are safe; 0.30.4 is malicious

malicious_packages.name: axios
malicious_packages.notes: Compromised via hijacked maintainer account (jasonsaayman). Injects plain-crypto-js@4.2.1 as a dependency. Both versions removed from npm registry by 03:29 UTC on 2026-03-31.

malicious_packages.name: plain-crypto-js
malicious_packages.notes: Cross-platform RAT payload. Harvests all env vars matching credential patterns and exfiltrates to sfrclak.com:8000 over HTTPS. Includes self-destruct mechanism to remove evidence. Runs via postinstall hook. npm security placeholder (0.0.1-security.0) published after takedown.

suspicious_packages.reason: Potential follow-up attack using axios brand

network_iocs.context: Primary C2/exfiltration endpoint. plain-crypto-js POSTs harvested credentials to sfrclak.com:8000
IP address backing sfrclak.com. Block outbound connections to this IP.
